arcanas.effect: Chance to freeze enemies on hit.

arcanas.name: Disco of Gold
arcanas.effect: Enemies drop more gold.
arcanas.unlockMethod: Reach minute 31 in any stage (or type code)

arcanas.name: Slash
arcanas.effect: Chance for critical hit based on speed.

arcanas.name: Lost and Found Painting
arcanas.effect: Revive once per run with 50% HP.

arcanas.name: Boogaloo of Illusions
arcanas.effect: Projectiles may split and bounce.

arcanas.name: Heart of Fire
arcanas.effect: Fire damage over time to nearby enemies.

arcanas.name: Silent Old Sanctuary
arcanas.effect: Weapons gain bonus effects at max level.
arcanas.unlockMethod: Reach minute 31 in any stage (or type code)

arcanas.name: Blood Astronomia
arcanas.effect: Killing enemies may reduce cooldowns.

arcanas.name: Stake to your Heart
arcanas.effect: Same as Game Killer (0) but stronger effect.

arcanas.name: Sapphire Mist
arcanas.effect: Same as Gemini (I) but clone attacks too.

arcanas.name: Hidden Anathema
arcanas.effect: Same as Tragic Princess (III) but poison spreads.

arcanas.name: Pale Diamond Incursion

arcanas.name: Moonlight Bolero
arcanas.effect: Same as Sarabande of Healing (VI) but stronger.

arcanas.name: Edge of the Earth
arcanas.effect: Same as Mad Groove (VIII) but items move faster.

arcanas.name: Hail from the Future
arcanas.effect: Same as Beginning (X) but also extra XP.

arcanas.name: Crystal Cries
arcanas.effect: Same as Out of Bounds (XII) but also more luck.

arcanas.name: Call of a Mad Moon
arcanas.effect: Same as Wicked Season (XIII) but starts stronger.

arcanas.name: Victorian Horror
arcanas.effect: Same as Boogaloo of Illusions (XVIII) but more bounces.

arcanas.name: Heir of Fate
arcanas.effect: Same as Heart of Fire (XIX) but also damage shield.

arcanas.name: Wandering the Jet Black
arcanas.effect: Same as Blood Astronomia (XXI) but better proc rate.

stages.name: Mad Forest
stages.description: Classic gothic forest stage. Great starting area.

stages.name: Inlaid Library
stages.description: Library with books and knowledge. Medium difficulty.

stages.name: Dairy Plant
stages.description: Abandoned dairy factory. Many industrial enemies.

stages.name: Gallo Tower
stages.description: Tall tower with aerial enemies and robed mages.

stages.name: Il Molise
stages.description: Rural Italian countryside. Easy stage.

stages.name: Whiteout
stages.description: Snowy frozen stage with ice-themed enemies.

stages.name: Laborratory
stages.description: High-tech laboratory with circuit-themed enemies.

stages.name: Westwood
stages.description: Overgrown ruins with plant and gold-themed enemies.

stages.name: The Coop
stages.description: Chicken coop with chicken enemies. 0 enemy kills unlocks Gaze of Gaea evolution.

stages.name: Space 54
stages.description: Outer space station with alien enemies.

stages.name: The Bone Zone
stages.description: Bone-filled hellscape. Endless skeleton enemies. Good for farming XP and骨头.

stages.name: Moongolow
stages.description: Moonlit ruins with Atlantean-themed enemies.

stages.name: Cappella Magna
stages.description: Final cathedral stage. Extremely difficult. Heavy demon and angel enemies.

stages.name: Astral Stair
stages.description: Psychedelic stairway to infinity. Cosmic and astral-themed enemies.

stages.name: Mazerella
stages.description: Dairy-themed stage with Torino enemies. Boss-heavy.

stages.name: Room 1665
stages.description: Deep dungeon with Darkasso enemies. 5-stage endless mode.

stages.name: Boss Rash
stages.description: Boss rush mode. Fight bosses back-to-back.

stages.name: Bat Country
stages.description: Bat-themed boss rush.

stages.name: Tiny Bridge
stages.description: Mini boss rush with varied enemies.

stages.name: Eudaimonia Machine
stages.description: Endless mode stage. The Directer boss fight in Phase 2.

stages.name: Mt. Moonspell
stages.description: Moonspell mountain with werewolf and moon-themed enemies.

stages.name: Lake Foscari
stages.description: Lake stage with aquatic and fancy-themed enemies.

stages.name: Polus Replica
stages.description: Among Us themed space station.

stages.name: Neo Galuga
stages.description: Green jungle ruins with updated enemy types.

stages.name: Hectic Highway
stages.description: Endless highway with vehicle-themed enemies.

stages.name: Ode to Castlevania
stages.description: Castlevania-themed stage with Castlevania enemies and 3 coffins.

stages.name: Emerald Diorama
stages.description: Gem-themed diorama stage.

stages.name: Ante Chamber
stages.description: Small mysterious chamber with card-themed enemies.

pickups.name: Sammys
pickups.notes: Can be chained for minutes of invulnerability

gameDesign.coreLoop: Move → Auto-Attack (weapons fire automatically) → Collect XP gems from killed enemies → Level up (choose 1 of 3 random weapons or passives) → Evolve weapons by collecting matching passives → Survive until the timer ends (typically 30:00).
weaponBehaviors: Primary weapons — Auto-fire in the faced direction (most common)
Aura weapons — Effect continuously around the player (Garlic, Laurel)
Bouncing/ricochet weapons — Projectiles ricochet off walls and enemies (Bone, Cherry Bomb)
Zone weapons — Place persistent damage zones on the ground (Santa Water, Fire Wand)
Orbit/circle weapons — Weapons circle around the player (King Bible, Peachone/Ebony Wings)
Retaliate weapons — Deal damage when the player is hit (Pako Battiliar, Victory Sword)
passiveStats: Luck — Better drop rates and proc chances
Greed — More gold from pickups and chests
Curse — More enemy spawns, but also higher enemy HP and speed
Recovery — Passive HP regeneration per second
gameDesign.difficultyScaling: Enemy HP and damage scale with time (affected by Curse stat). More enemies spawn as time progresses. Different enemy tiers appear at different minutes (e.g., basic enemies at 1-3 min, elites at 10+ min). Boss enemies appear at fixed minutes (11:00, 21:00) and in boss rush modes. Stage-specific difficulty: Mad Forest is tutorial-easy, Cappella Magna is the hardest base stage.
gameDesign.economyDesign: XP gems are color-coded by value (green → red = low → high). Gold is earned from pickups and defeated enemies; used for shop purchases and rerolls in runs that have a shop. Duplicator pickup adds +1 projectile permanently to all weapons for the run. Reroll currency (gold) is scarce early, plentiful late. Pickups provide meaningful build decisions vs passive stat sticks.
